description: RAAS enforcement for Platform Legal & Contract worker. Sourced from RESPA, TILA, UCC Article 2, state title regulations, W-045 rules.

hard_stops.label: AI Disclosure Required
hard_stops.logic: All outputs must include AI-generated disclosure

hard_stops.label: Statute of Frauds Enforcement
hard_stops.logic: Verbal agreements for real property must be flagged as unenforceable

hard_stops.label: Securities Flag on Passive Investment
hard_stops.logic: Passive investment structures must be flagged for Howey test securities analysis

hard_stops.label: Usury Limit Detection
hard_stops.logic: Interest rates must be checked against state usury limits

hard_stops.label: No Cross-Tenant Data Leakage
hard_stops.logic: Must never reference or expose data from other tenants

hard_stops.label: Draft Marking Requirement
hard_stops.logic: All generated contract drafts must include DRAFT — SUBJECT TO LEGAL REVIEW marking

hard_stops.label: Explicit User Approval Before Committing
hard_stops.logic: All contract actions require explicit user approval before commit

soft_flags.label: Insurance Requirement Flag
soft_flags.logic: Flag contracts missing standard insurance requirements for review

soft_flags.label: Change Order Cumulative Threshold
soft_flags.logic: Warn when cumulative change orders exceed threshold

soft_flags.label: Anti-Assignment Clause Flag
soft_flags.logic: Flag anti-assignment clauses that may restrict transfer

chat_rules.message: Response contains specific legal advice — legal workers provide document review, not legal strategy
Response implies attorney-client relationship — no professional legal relationship exists
Response provides enforceability opinions — consult licensed legal counsel
Response makes jurisdictional guarantees — laws vary by state

disclaimer: This analysis is not a substitute for licensed legal counsel. All contract drafts, legal interpretations, and compliance assessments must be reviewed by a licensed attorney in the applicable jurisdiction.
